summarization.a: The figure illustrates how resistivity decreases as deposition temperature increases. Measurements from spectroscopic ellipsometry (solid line) and 4-point probe (open circles) both show a consistent downward trend, indicating improved conductivity at higher deposition temperatures. Error bars represent experimental uncertainty for each temperature point.

| 200                         | 150                  | Spectroscopic ellipsometry |
| 300                         | 120                  | Spectroscopic ellipsometry |
| 400                         | 80                   | Spectroscopic ellipsometry |
| 100                         | 200                  | 4-point probe              |
| 400                         | 70                   | 4-point probe              |

b.question: What property is being measured in this plot?
Which measurement techniques were used to obtain resistivity data?
Does the deposition temperature significantly influence the electrical resistivity?

a.question: How does resistivity change with increasing deposition temperature?
a.answer: Resistivity decreases steadily as deposition temperature rises, showing that higher temperatures improve the film’s conductivity due to better atomic ordering or reduced defects.
